item.title: The consequences of online race hatred are playing out on Belfast’s streets
item.description: Malign social media automates division — I saw the effects in our city this week

item.title: Frasers is better at making headlines than creating shareholder value
item.description: Mike Ashley’s penchant for stake-building is not that well suited to the public markets

item.title: How to tame the inflation python
item.description: A ‘wait and see’ monetary policy strategy risks repeating the mistakes made in 2008

item.title: How AI is disrupting investment
item.description: The technology is leading to a fundamental shift in the way investors allocate funds and diversify risks across every asset class

item.title: How to speak Fifa
item.description: A user’s guide to interpreting global football chief Gianni Infantino’s most inspirational thoughts

item.title: European tech sovereignty: a doubtful goal but a good investment
item.description: Given the continent’s weak starting point, its modest ambitions could have a big impact on local companies

item.title: Forget the football — Grand Theft Auto can unite the world
item.description: Thirteen years in the making, the game’s next instalment promises to be the biggest entertainment product in history

item.title: Is privacy the latest luxury?
item.description: Why being offline and unseen is becoming a status symbol

item.title: It’s time to celebrate Chenin Blanc
item.description: The wines are appetising, versatile partners for food and rarely overpriced

item.title: Little Baobab, London: ‘A place you want to be’ — restaurant review
item.description: At this Senegalese restaurant in the Africa Centre, every element — from mood and service to comforting, powerful food — combines to soulwarming effect

item.title: ‘Rivals’ may be racy — but ITV’s real-life franchise wars were stranger still
item.description: For veterans of Britain’s broadcast licence battles, the Disney+ Jilly Cooper adaptation paints a recognisable picture

item.title: Football will eclipse politics at this World Cup
item.description: Goals, talent and diversity are set to drown out controversy off the pitch

item.description: His unrivalled grip on the public imagination has transmuted into Wall Street gold

item.description: Resignation of UK defence secretary shows some governments will struggle to find the money

item.description: It might be improper to put the president’s face on the currency. It might also not work

item.title: Sooner or later, America is going to have to reckon with the debt
item.description: There is a bigger political constituency for fiscal consolidation than many think

item.description: Better patient outcomes are at risk in the backlash against American tech

item.description: The X owner is using his platform to amplify extremist opponents of multiculturalism in Britain

item.title: Why do employers think it’s OK to ghost job applicants?
item.description: Businesses that complain it is hard to recruit the right staff should look at themselves too

item.description: Relations between the politicians — both suspicious towards each other — sank to new lows after PM’s mocking comment

item.description: Plus debt restructurings and de-dollarisation

item.title: Musk should be bolder with his fantastical projections
item.description: Financial history offers examples of now-giant companies that formerly lowballed their own prospects

item.title: The great bond and equity conundrum
item.description: Financial logic has been stood on its head as yields buck decades-long trends

item.title: Japan’s PM: Building resilience in a turbulent world
item.description: To tackle the challenges of today, it is essential to acquire new capabilities
